industries.name: Private Banks
industries.kpi: ROA >1.5%, NIM, GNPA/NNPA, CASA, CAR (not scraped — manual review)
industries.note: Banks ranked on ROE + profit growth; ROCE/OPM/D-E are not meaningful for lenders.

industries.name: PSU Banks
industries.kpi: ROA >0.9%, GNPA/NNPA, CASA, CAR, P/B (not scraped)
industries.note: Ranked on ROE + profit growth. Verify asset-quality on screener.in before use.

industries.name: NBFCs
industries.kpi: ROA, NIM/spread, GNPA/NNPA, cost-to-income, CAR, AUM growth (not scraped)
industries.note: Ranked on ROE + profit growth.

industries.name: Housing Finance
industries.kpi: ROA, spread, GNPA/NNPA, CAR, AUM growth (not scraped)
industries.note: Ranked on ROE + profit growth.

top3.name: Aptus Value Housing Finance India Ltd

industries.name: Life Insurance
industries.kpi: VNB margin, APE growth, persistency, RoEV (not scraped)
industries.note: Screener metrics are weak proxies for insurers — treat ranking as indicative only.

top3.name: ICICI Prudential Life Insurance Company Ltd

industries.name: General & Health Insurance
industries.kpi: Combined ratio <100%, solvency, loss ratio, GWP growth (not scraped)
industries.note: Screener metrics are weak proxies for insurers — indicative only.

top3.name: ICICI Lombard General Insurance Company Ltd
Star Health & Allied Insurance Company Ltd

industries.name: Capital Markets & AMCs
industries.kpi: ROE >25%, debt-free, AUM/volume growth, operating margin
industries.note: Asset-light — the general quality filter works well here.

top3.name: Multi Commodity Exchange of India Ltd

industries.name: IT / Software Services
industries.kpi: Constant-currency growth, EBIT margin >20%, attrition, deal TCV, FCF conversion
industries.note: Mega-caps often miss the 12% profit-CAGR bar; mid-caps score on growth.

industries.name: FMCG
industries.kpi: Volume growth, OPM >18%, ROCE >25%, cash conversion
industries.note: Sector-wide growth is soft; several elite-return names miss the growth filter.

industries.name: Pharma
industries.kpi: OPM >18%, D/E <0.5, USFDA status, R&D pipeline
industries.note: Check USFDA inspection status separately — not on screener.

industries.name: Hospitals & Diagnostics
industries.kpi: ARPOB, occupancy, bed additions (hospitals); ROCE, network (diagnostics)
industries.note: Expansion capex can depress trailing ROCE for the fastest growers.

industries.name: Auto OEMs
industries.kpi: Volume growth, market-share trend, EBITDA margin, EV readiness
industries.note: Captive-finance arms distort consolidated ROCE/D-E for some OEMs.

industries.name: Auto Ancillaries
industries.kpi: Content per vehicle, client diversification, export %, ROCE
industries.note: Export % not on screener — verify separately.

industries.name: Metals & Mining
industries.kpi: EV/EBITDA, cost-per-tonne, net-debt/EBITDA <3, capacity utilisation
industries.note: Cyclical — trailing returns near cycle peak; ranked on relative strength + balance sheet.

industries.name: Cement
industries.kpi: EBITDA/tonne, capacity utilisation >70%, realisations, regional strength
industries.note: Cyclical — fresh off a pricing trough; few names pass the full filter.

industries.name: Capital Goods & Engineering
industries.kpi: Order book & book-to-bill >2.5x, order inflow, execution, working capital
industries.note: Order book not on screener — verify from company disclosures.

industries.name: Defence
industries.kpi: Order book & book-to-bill, indigenisation %, order inflow
industries.note: Multi-year order visibility not captured by screener ratios.

industries.name: Power & Utilities
industries.kpi: PLF/capacity utilisation, regulated vs merchant, receivable days, RE pipeline
industries.note: Regulated-return model structurally caps ROCE — most names fail the filter (not a defect).

industries.name: Oil & Gas / Energy
industries.kpi: GRM (OMCs), crude leverage (upstream), volume growth & margin/scm (CGD)
industries.note: Commodity/regulated — upstream & CGD often sit at 12-14% ROE.

industries.name: Building Materials
industries.kpi: Brand strength, OPM, ROCE >18%, distribution, volume growth
industries.note: Construction soft patch has softened trailing ratios for some names.

industries.name: Telecom
industries.kpi: ARPU & trend, subscriber/market share, net debt, EBITDA margin
industries.note: Carriers carry heavy absolute debt; tower/infra layer scores cleanest.

industries.name: Media & Entertainment
industries.kpi: FCF, balance sheet, content/IP ownership (be selective)
industries.note: Most of the sector destroys capital; winners are debt-free IP owners.

industries.name: Logistics & Ports
industries.kpi: Throughput growth, asset turns, EBITDA/unit, network density
industries.note: Ports are high-margin; surface transport is asset-turn driven.

top3.name: Adani Ports & Special Economic Zone Ltd

industries.name: Hotels & Tourism
industries.kpi: RevPAR, occupancy, ARR, room additions
industries.note: Mid/late upcycle; asset-heavy base depresses ROE — prefer low-debt owners.

industries.name: Aviation
industries.kpi: Load factor, RASK/CASK spread, fleet, balance sheet (usually weak)
industries.note: Only one structurally profitable listed carrier — be honest about thin depth.

industries.name: Textiles
industries.kpi: Cost position, capacity utilisation, debt, export/value-add mix
industries.note: Favour low-cost, high-value-add, debt-light exporters over commodity spinners.

industries.name: Real Estate
industries.kpi: Pre-sales/bookings growth (lead), net debt, collections — use NAV not P/E
industries.note: Revenue-recognition lag makes ROCE/ROE fail by design — that is normal here.

industries.name: Consumer Durables & Electricals
industries.kpi: Volume growth, premiumisation, OPM, working capital, ROCE
industries.note: Cables/wires and cooling names lead on returns + growth.

industries.name: Electronics Manufacturing (EMS)
industries.kpi: Order book, client wins, revenue growth, asset turns, ROCE (thin margins are structural)
industries.note: Judge on ROCE + asset turns + growth, not margin.

industries.name: Specialty Chemicals
industries.kpi: ROCE >18%, OPM >18%, R&D, China+1 wins, capex runway, asset turns
industries.note: FY23-26 down-cycle depresses trailing growth for several quality names.
